subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Lebanese independent press (L'Orient-Le Jour, Megaphone, TIMEP) + HRW/Amnesty/UNHCR.

Scored on corroborated facts: the 2019 financial collapse and banking insolvency, the 2024 Hezbollah-Israel war that devastated the south, the early-2025 filling of a two-year presidential vacuum (a reform window), and high re-escalation risk. Any specific renewed-2026-war reports are treated as elevated risk, not stated history.

Standout drags: an insolvent frozen banking system, a defaulted state, sectarian-captured institutions, a war-wrecked south, and collapsed infrastructure; the upside is a fragile 2025 reform window. Overall: negative, near worse than long.

economic.notes: A defaulted state and an insolvent, frozen banking system, with no productive base.

fiscal_state.notes: Defaulted in 2020 with collapsed state finances and no credible budget, against an unimplemented 2025 reform-government attempt. (World Bank; IMF)

monetary_independence.notes: A currency collapse, de-facto dollarization, and a discredited central bank. (State Dept ICS; Freedom House)

inflation.notes: A hyperinflation episode now easing as the economy dollarizes. (World Bank)

banking_stability.notes: The banking system is insolvent and frozen - depositors locked out since 2019 with no enforced resolution. (TIMEP; Bloomberg)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Import-dependent and remittance/diaspora-reliant with no real productive base and war damage. (World Bank)

institutional.notes: Sectarian-captured and paralysed, with a relatively strong independent press the exception.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak and sectarian-captured with impunity (the Beirut-port-blast investigation long blocked), against a cautious 2025-26 judicial revival. (HRW; TIMEP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak and sparse official data. (IMF data-gap notes)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Collapsed, clientelist, and paralysed by sectarian quotas, hollowed by exodus. (HRW)

press_freedom.notes: Regionally pluralistic with a strong independent press, against impunity and journalists targeted in conflict. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A fragile confessional system with a 2025 reform window, against deep cleavages, refugee strain, and brain drain.

political_stability.notes: A confessional power-sharing system that left a two-year presidential vacuum, filled in early 2025 (a reform window after the 2024 Hezbollah-Israel war), but remains deeply fragile with high re-escalation risk. (Al Jazeera; House of Commons Library)

civil_liberties.notes: Formally freer than regional peers but eroding under security pressure. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Among the world's highest refugee loads per capita under severe strain and official pushback, with no path to belonging. (UNHCR; TCF)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep sectarian cleavages, a Hezbollah-state duality, and fresh war trauma. (HRW; CFR)

demographics.notes: Mass emigration and brain drain of the young and professional. (WHO; brain-drain studies)

geopolitical.notes: A fractured state with an armed state-within-a-state and acute war exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A fractured state with an Iran-aligned armed movement operating as a state-within-a-state and Western/Gulf aid conditional - unreliable. (Foreign Policy; State Dept)

conflict_involvement.notes: The 2024 Hezbollah-Israel war devastated the south; a fragile ceasefire holds amid ongoing Israeli strikes and disarmament friction, with high re-escalation risk - a real war tail. (Al Jazeera; House of Commons Library)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Informal capital controls (the depositor lockout), US sanctions on the armed movement, and grey-list risk. (Arab News)

physical_practical.notes: Collapsed infrastructure and a degraded health system, with seismic exposure.

climate.notes: Eastern-Med drought/heat, water stress, snowpack decline, with readiness gutted by state collapse and fiscal crisis. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Once-strong, now degraded and brain-drained, with drug shortages and war-damaged hospitals. (WHO; MSF)

infrastructure.notes: Collapsed - chronic blackouts and a private-generator economy, water failures, and war damage. (HRW)

crime_safety.notes: Rising with economic desperation, though not catastrophic. (Freedom House)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible coverage, with seismic-fault exposure. (hazard context)

summary: A state in structural collapse: defaulted since 2020, an insolvent banking system that has locked depositors out since 2019, a currency that lost most of its value, de-facto dollarization, and no productive base. Institutions are sectarian-captured and paralysed - though the independent press remains relatively strong - and society is fragmented by deep confessional cleavages, a Hezbollah-state duality, mass brain-drain emigration, and among the world's highest refugee loads. The 2024 war with Israel devastated the south, with a fragile ceasefire, ongoing strikes, and disarmament friction leaving high re-escalation risk; the lone upside is a fragile early-2025 reform window.
